safest_plan.entry_basis: Chart 0.5 fib pullback (blue line labelled '0.5', image) with green demand line ~26.00 just below; deeper clean buy is the 'OK AREA' ~24.00 (image, estimated). Current price 28.97 is mid-range at the value area, not a low-risk entry.

targets.basis: Green circled value-area rejection line ~30.00 (image) — first partial / supply check
Grey supply zone ~32.5–33.2 / previous swing high (image) — extended target, matches Zero grade 'supply zone above' + 'previous swing high'

safest_plan.stop_basis: Below the green demand line ~26.00 (image). For a continuation entry at current price the source's stop is a close below the POC / value-area structure (~28.4, image).

stance_by_source.summary: About a 27% bounce off the ~$24 lows is already captured; now cautiously managing continuation — wants the stock to hold the value area and push into the supply/previous swing high above, invalid on a close back below that value area.

conflicts: The extracted text carried no numeric levels — all prices here are read off the chart image (value area ~28.5, 0.5 fib ~26.50, supply ~30 and ~32.5–33, OK buy ~24).
The chart's own hand-drawn projection expects a pullback toward ~26.50/26.00 before continuation, which argues against chasing the current ~$29 price even though the source's stance is bullish.
plain_english: Carnival has already bounced about 27% off its lows near $24 and is now stalled around $29, where it keeps getting pushed back. The single source here is cautiously positive — but the easy move is done; it wants the stock to hold this area and grind toward the $30, then $32.50–33 resistance, and would be proven wrong if it falls back and closes under roughly $28.50. The lower-risk plan is to wait for a dip toward $26.50 (support at $26, deeper buy near $24) rather than buy here. Action: wait — either for a confirmed hold and push above $29, or for that pullback, before entering.
